title: oligonucleotide_ribotac_cross_v1 — CROSS-AXIS W3 output rows (OLIGONUCLEOTIDE x RIBOTAC)
description: Output schema for _python_bridge/module/oligonucleotide_ribotac_cross.py. CROSS-AXIS W3: the OLIGONUCLEOTIDE axis (antisense oligonucleotide / siRNA, SantaLucia 1998 nearest-neighbor duplex thermodynamics) is run side-by-side with the RIBOTAC sub-axis (Disney-lab class, mass-action bifunctional recruitment of endogenous RNase-L for catalytic multi-turnover cleavage). Same RNA-target CLASS (both modalities target RNA), opposite TURNOVER REGIME (ASO is STOICHIOMETRIC — one ASO molecule blocks at most one transcript via RNase-H recruitment or steric block, gets consumed; RIBOTAC is CATALYTIC multi-turnover — one RIBOTAC turns over many RNA molecules via recruited RNase-L). Both parent simulators are IMPORTED, never reimplemented (governance f3). The PASS sentinel certifies IN-SILICO simulator+metadata internal consistency ONLY (g8/f2) — never a therapeutic, clinical, regulatory, immunogenic, knockdown, efficacy, or modality-superiority claim. CRITICAL HONESTY: this is a COMPARISON, NOT A RANKING. The two model signals are NOT commensurable (Tm in deg C and N_RNA_blocked for the stoichiometric ASO path vs Kd/k_cat in nM/s and N_RNA_cleaved for the catalytic RIBOTAC path — different units on different real limits). Nusinersen / patisiran / inclisiran (FDA-approved ASO and siRNA drugs) and Disney-lab RIBOTACs (research-stage, NO FDA-approved RIBOTAC) are NOT competing modalities to be ranked here — they target different RNA biology questions. Modalities are described by their own precedent (g3/f1/f_lattice_fit — never lattice-derived). OLIGONUCLEOTIDE is an expansion-main axis (NOT core-5); RIBOTAC is a SUB-AXIS :> BIFUNCTIONAL (also NOT core-5). The cross does NOT create a new axis.

row_kind.description: Which axis pathway this row represents. The two pathways share the same RNA-target CLASS but differ in TURNOVER REGIME — they are NOT collapsed into a single rate.

modality.description: Modality label. OLIGONUCLEOTIDE = antisense oligonucleotide / siRNA path; RIBOTAC = bifunctional RNase-L-recruiting catalytic path.

target_name.description: Panel target identifier — paired between the OLIGONUCLEOTIDE and RIBOTAC rows for the same RNA target.

paired_target_name.description: Echo of target_name used to assert OLIGONUCLEOTIDE + RIBOTAC rows are paired per target.

rna_target_class.description: The shared RNA-target class (e.g. 'structured RNA target — pre-miRNA / structured hairpin'). Common ground is the SHARED TARGET CLASS, NOT a shared metric.

turnover_regime.description: Turnover regime — the axis on which the two pathways disagree. OLIGONUCLEOTIDE row: STOICHIOMETRIC (N_RNA_blocked = N_ASO_molecules; one ASO blocks at most one transcript via RNase-H recruitment or steric block, then is consumed). RIBOTAC row: CATALYTIC (N_RNA_cleaved = k_cat * t_exposure; one RIBOTAC molecule recruits RNase-L for many turnovers).

turnover_regime_description.description: Human-readable description of the turnover regime for this row.

drug_precedent.description: Own modality precedent (g3/f1/f_lattice_fit — never lattice-derived). OLIGONUCLEOTIDE: nusinersen / Spinraza (ASO, FDA 2016, intrathecal SMN2 splice-switching), patisiran / Onpattro (siRNA, FDA 2018, hereditary ATTR amyloidosis), inclisiran / Leqvio (siRNA, FDA 2021, hypercholesterolemia). RIBOTAC: Disney-lab RNase-L-recruiting bifunctional class (Costales 2018 JACS 140:6741; Costales 2020 PNAS 117:2406) — RESEARCH-STAGE, no FDA-approved RIBOTAC.

modality_stage.description: Development stage of the modality. OLIGONUCLEOTIDE: approved (multiple FDA-approved ASO and siRNA drugs since 2016). RIBOTAC: research-stage (no approved RIBOTAC).

mechanism.description: How this modality acts on the RNA target.

model_signal_name.description: Name of the in-silico model-computed signal for this modality. The two signals are NOT commensurable across rows.

model_signal_value.description: The model-computed signal value. NOT comparable across modalities — the OLIGONUCLEOTIDE row reports Tm (deg C) + N_RNA_blocked; the RIBOTAC row reports Kd (nM) + N_RNA_cleaved. Different units on different real limits.

model_signal_detail.description: Modality-specific breakdown of the model signal. For OLIGONUCLEOTIDE: SantaLucia NN thermodynamics (dH, dS, dG37, Tm) + stoichiometric N_blocked. For RIBOTAC: mass-action ternary occupancy (Kd1, Kd2, alpha, ternary_fraction) + catalytic N_cleaved = k_cat * t_exposure.

n_rna_neutralized_per_drug_molecule.description: RNA copies neutralized per drug molecule under this modality's mechanism. OLIGONUCLEOTIDE (stoichiometric) bounded above by 1.0 per ASO event; RIBOTAC (catalytic) equal to ternary_fraction * turnover_number (multi-turnover advantage). VALUES ARE NOT COMMENSURABLE across rows — they measure different physical events (transcript-blocking vs RNase-L-cleavage).

real_limit_anchor.description: The real physical/thermodynamic limit this modality's signal is anchored to (g1 — real-limits-first). OLIGONUCLEOTIDE: nucleic-acid duplex hybridization thermodynamics (nearest-neighbor base-pair stacking free energy bounds duplex stability). RIBOTAC: catalytic multiple-turnover (a genuine catalyst has turnover number > 1) + mass-action law (bound fractions <= 1).

real_limit_citations.description: Non-empty literature citation list for the modality's real-limit anchor(s). OLIGONUCLEOTIDE: SantaLucia 1998 PNAS 95:1460. RIBOTAC: Costales 2018 JACS 140:6741, Costales 2020 PNAS 117:2406, Cornish-Bowden 2012 Fundamentals of Enzyme Kinetics 4e, Guldberg & Waage 1864 (mass action).

lattice_stance.description: Explicit statement that no n=6 lattice arithmetic is used in this row (g2 lattice-is-tool, g3 honesty-external, f1 / f_lattice_fit). No Tm / dG / Kd / k_cat / N here is derived from sigma/tau/phi/J2.

comparison_is_ranking.description: MUST be false. This deliverable is a descriptive side-by-side comparison; it is NEVER a ranking of the modalities or drugs. Nusinersen/patisiran/inclisiran (FDA-approved) and Disney-lab RIBOTACs (research-stage) are not competing modalities to be ranked here.

signals_commensurable.description: MUST be false. The OLIGONUCLEOTIDE model signal (Tm in deg C + N_blocked, stoichiometric) and the RIBOTAC model signal (Kd in nM + N_cleaved, catalytic) live in DIFFERENT UNITS on DIFFERENT real limits and CANNOT be ranked against each other.

illustrative_only.description: MUST be true. Sequences, K_d / k_cat / alpha / t_exposure values and Tm anchors are illustrative literature-informed surrogates — NOT fits to a specific compound and NOT a wet-lab measurement (g8/f2).

creates_a_new_axis.description: MUST be false. This cross does NOT create a new axis. The core-5 axes (QUANTUM / WEAVE / NANOBOT / RIBOZYME / VIROCAPSID) are UNCHANGED; OLIGONUCLEOTIDE remains an expansion-main axis and RIBOTAC remains a sub-axis :> BIFUNCTIONAL (also an expansion-main).

in_silico_only.description: MUST be true — the row certifies in-silico simulator+metadata consistency only (g8/f2), never a therapeutic / clinical / regulatory / immunogenic / knockdown / efficacy claim.

oligo_sequence_5to3.description: [oligonucleotide row only] ASO sequence 5'->3' (DNA alphabet; U->T accepted by the parent NN engine). The ASO is the reverse complement of the RNA-target region it would hybridize.

oligo_length_nt.description: [oligonucleotide row only] Length of the ASO (nt).

duplex_dH_kcal_mol.description: [oligonucleotide row only] Duplex enthalpy from the SantaLucia 1998 unified NN sum (kcal/mol).

duplex_dS_cal_mol_K.description: [oligonucleotide row only] Duplex entropy from the SantaLucia 1998 unified NN sum (cal/mol/K).

duplex_dG37_kcal_mol.description: [oligonucleotide row only] Duplex free energy at 37 deg C (kcal/mol).

duplex_Tm_celsius.description: [oligonucleotide row only] van 't Hoff two-state Tm at 0.4 uM strand, 1 M Na+ (deg C). 1 M Na+ is the SantaLucia standard state and is non-physiological.

n_aso_molecules.description: [oligonucleotide row only] Illustrative ASO-molecule count used to compute the stoichiometric N_RNA_blocked under the 1-ASO-blocks-1-transcript ceiling.

n_rna_blocked_stoichiometric.description: [oligonucleotide row only] N_RNA_blocked under the stoichiometric model = N_aso_molecules (one ASO blocks at most one transcript; the ASO is consumed / sequestered). Real-limit floor: stoichiometric binder has turnover number = 1 by definition.

rna_target_seq.description: [ribotac row only] RNA-target sequence (A/C/G/U) for the RIBOTAC pathway.

rna_length_nt.description: [ribotac row only] Length of the RNA target (nt).

dot_bracket.description: [ribotac row only] Vienna dot-bracket secondary structure of the target RNA (parent RIBOZYME-axis Nussinov solver, imported via ribotac_sim — no fork at any remove).

num_base_pairs.description: [ribotac row only] Number of base pairs in the dot-bracket structure.

k_d1_nM.description: [ribotac row only] Dissociation constant of the RNA-binding warhead to the target (nM).

k_d2_nM.description: [ribotac row only] Intrinsic dissociation constant of the recruiter warhead to RNase-L (nM).

alpha.description: [ribotac row only] Linker-tethered ternary cooperativity factor (>= 1).

k_d2_effective_nM.description: [ribotac row only] Effective second-site Kd = K_d2 / alpha (nM).

binary_fraction.description: [ribotac row only] Mass-action occupancy of the binary Target.R complex (Guldberg & Waage 1864 real-limit; bounded by 1.0).

rnase_site_fraction.description: [ribotac row only] Mass-action occupancy of the RNase-L recruiter site given the binary complex.

ternary_fraction.description: [ribotac row only] Ternary Target.R.RNase-L fraction = binary_fraction * rnase_site_fraction.

k_cat_per_s.description: [ribotac row only] Recruited-RNase-L catalytic turnover rate for the bound RNA (1/s).

t_exposure_s.description: [ribotac row only] Exposure window over which turnovers accumulate (s).

turnover_number.description: [ribotac row only] Catalytic turnover number N = k_cat * t_exposure. Real-limit: a genuine catalyst has N > 1 (Cornish-Bowden 2012).

n_rna_cleaved_catalytic.description: [ribotac row only] N_RNA_cleaved = k_cat * t_exposure (the catalytic multiple-turnover count per RIBOTAC molecule). Real-limit: catalytic turnover > 1 (vs stoichiometric N = 1).

is_catalytic.description: [ribotac row only] True iff turnover_number > 1 — the defining RIBOTAC catalytic property.
